SDG 1

Kadir Has University actively contributes to eradicating poverty through both social engagement and academic research.  Student teams collaborated with the Basic Needs Association (TİDER) to support municipal food banks in Şişli and Beyoğlu, receiving training on urban poverty and food waste before volunteering in daily operations. Faculty-led TÜBİTAK 3501 research explored cooperation under resource scarcity, and publications addressed sustainable income support policies in Turkey. In addition, initiatives like the Zorlu PSM Theatre Workshop provided free professional training to aspiring artists from disadvantaged backgrounds, promoting cultural participation and social inclusion.

1.1. Events

The Psychological Origins of Cooperation

[1]

The Psychological Origins of Cooperation (

PDF

) was supported by a training activity conducted within the Faculty of Art and Design, Theatre Department through the Zorlu PSM Workshop, held between 10 January and 31 May 2023. The program featured expert instructors Serdar Biliş, Özlem Hemiş, and Özlem Özhabes, and targeted writers, directors, and producers who applied as scholarship recipients. Offered free of charge, the PSM Workshop aims to introduce new talents to the theatre and enrich original content.

[2]

1.4. Courses

KHAS110 – Social Responsibility Project was carried out in cooperation with Temel İhtiyaç Derneği (Tider), where student teams volunteered at food banks operated by the Şişli and Beyoğlu Municipalities’ Social Services Directorates. Prior to their assignments, students received training on food waste and urban poverty, after which they engaged in hands-on volunteer work, including shelf-stacking, assisting visitors, and storehouse duties.

KH542 – Current Issues in International Tax Law
